ci-flake-rekick.l0: A flake → re-kick (max 2×); never if the diff touches the failing area.
ci-flake-rekick.l1: Classifier precedence is infra > out-of-scope > flake > real_bug. Only call it a flake when the failure is known-intermittent or passes on re-run AND is unrelated to the change. If the diff touches the failing area it is a real_bug, not a flake.
ci-flake-rekick.value: Misclassifying wastes money and thrashes the branch. See src/dev-mesh/classify.js. infra_auth (auth/secret/network, or claude died in <2s before any test ran) → escalate to a human, never 'fix'. out_of_scope (reproduces on base) → report, don't edit.

container-signing-flake.l0: change-detect.test.js failures in the sandbox are a known git-signing infra flake.
container-signing-flake.l1: In the ephemeral container, git commit signing is unavailable, so change-detect's git-porcelain path fails ~4 subtests. This is environmental, not a regression — the suite is otherwise green.
container-signing-flake.value: When triaging a red run, if the ONLY failure is change-detect.test.js with signing/porcelain errors, classify infra_auth (environment), not real_bug. Confirm the same passes in CI (ci.yml) where signing is configured.
